title: dacryodes incurvata
intro: Dacryodes incurvata, also known as the African walnut or African oil palm, is a tropical plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and West Africa. The medicinal properties of Dacryodes incurvata include its antiseptic, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Dacryodes incurvata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional African medicine.
